derivation: PACA max transit 14 days = 2 weeks for non-refrigerated. Model shelf life thirds: 0-8wk fresh, 8-16wk mid, 16-24wk near_expiry. At warm rate 0.28/step, 50% of near_expiry stock spoils in 2.5 steps. Calibrated so a unit placed in near_expiry clears in ~3-4 steps ≈ 3-4 weeks warm.
handbook_66_reference: 2-3 week shelf life at ambient for table stock

unit: multiplier applied to all aging transition rates
derivation: ARS Handbook 66: cold storage extends shelf life from ~3 weeks (warm) to 5-8 months (cold). Ratio: ~(6 months)/(3 weeks) ≈ 8×. Model cold factor 1/0.35 ≈ 2.9× slower — conservative relative to maximum, representing active commercial cold-chain rather than ideal long-term storage.
paca_reference: Transit temperature spec 4-10°C; acceptable delivery up to 14 days

derivation: Calibrated so fresh inventory transitions to mid_life over ~5-6 steps (5-6 weeks). This places new-harvest potatoes in mid_life condition by week 5-6 in warm storage, consistent with market quality observations.
paca_reference: N/A (internal cohort boundary, not a PACA threshold)
handbook_66_reference: Sprouting begins 2-4 weeks at warm temperature

derivation: Calibrated so mid_life transitions to near_expiry over ~4-5 steps. Combined with fresh_to_mid: a unit of fresh inventory becomes near_expiry after approximately 8-10 steps (8-10 weeks) at warm storage.
paca_reference: 14-day good-delivery window implies arrival quality constraint
handbook_66_reference: Quality deterioration accelerates after 6-8 weeks ambient
